description: Pre-Session B classification for spirit (reg 184). Sets anchor verse Eze 36:27. Reviews pre-extracted terms. Identifies H7307I ru.ach:side (spatial preposition, no spirit content) for reclassification. Confirms H5397 ne.sha.mah as extracted. Flags H7307J ru.ach:temper as cross-registry shared term with anger. Confirms candidate_delete for bleed vocabulary. Documents H4152 pneumatikos as candidate_delete requiring review.

operations.description: Set anchor verse for spirit registry. Eze 36:27 — 'And I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes and be careful to obey my rules.' Most comprehensive statement of the Spirit's indwelling work in the human person. Directly addresses the heart-spirit boundary (with Eze 36:26), the soul-spirit question (the new spirit placed within the ne.phesh-person), and the Spirit as the agent of moral transformation. Resolves multiple PH2 flags from soul, heart, and fear registries.

H7307I ru.ach [spirit: side] — reclassify extracted → candidate_delete. All 7 verses are spatial directional: east/north/south/west side of temple measurements (Eze 42:16-20), gatekeepers on four sides (1Ch 9:24), pomegranates on the sides (Jer 52:23). This is ru.ach as a directional preposition, carrying zero spirit/breath/wind content. No inner-being vocabulary.

set.status_note: Spatial directional sub-gloss only. All 7 verses are temple/architectural measurement contexts. No spirit/breath/inner-being content.

operations.description: H7307J ru.ach [spirit: temper] — confirm extracted. Cross-registry shared term with anger (reg 4). 10 verses: Pro 14:29 (hasty spirit/temper), Pro 16:32 (he who rules his spirit), Pro 17:27 (cool spirit = understanding), Pro 29:11 (fool gives full vent to his spirit), Ecc 7:9 (quick in spirit to become angry). The temper/disposition sub-gloss captures the spirit as the seat of the person's inner disposition — which can be hasty, cool, humble, or proud. Shared term with anger registry confirmed.

set.status_note: Confirmed extracted. Cross-registry SHARED_TERM with anger (reg 4). The disposition/temper dimension of ru.ach — spirit as the seat of inner disposition and emotional register.

operations.description: G4152 pneumatikos [spiritual] — reclassify candidate_delete → extracted. 28 occurrences. Analytically essential for the two-modes structure: 1Co 15:44-46 (psuchikon soma vs pneumatikon soma — natural body vs spiritual body) is the most important two-modes statement in the NT. pneumatikos describes the Spirit-constituted mode of existence. Also: Gal 6:1 (you who are spiritual), Eph 1:3 (spiritual blessings), Eph 6:12 (spiritual forces of evil). Currently has 0 verses in this export — Claude Code must populate verse data before Session B closure.

set.status_note: Essential for two-modes structure (psuchikos/pneumatikos distinction). Requires verse data population before analysis completion.

operations.description: H5397 ne.sha.mah [breath] — confirm extracted (corrected from candidate_delete). 24 verses with full refs. Gen 2:7 anchor: 'breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living being.' Job 32:8 — 'the breath (ne.sha.mah) of the Almighty makes him understand.' Job 33:4 — 'The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.' Pro 20:27 — 'the spirit of man is the lamp of the Lord.' Psa 150:6 — 'let everything that has breath praise the Lord.' Ne.sha.mah and ru.ach together form the dual vocabulary for the divine breath/spirit of life. Ne.sha.mah is more specifically the animating breath of life; ru.ach is the broader spirit/wind/breath vocabulary.

set.status_note: Confirmed extracted. Gen 2:7 anchor for divine breath of life. With ru.ach, forms the OT dual vocabulary for the animating spirit-breath. Ne.sha.mah = specifically the breath that animates; ru.ach = broader spirit/wind/breath.

operations.description: Bulk set None-status bleed terms → delete. The 46 None-status terms are entirely cross-registry bleed from: heart vocabulary (lev/le.vav/kardia/lib.bah), soul vocabulary (ne.phesh sub-glosses G+H+I+J+K+L+M+N, psuchē sub-glosses G+H+I+J+K, psuchikos, oligopsuchos, dipsuchos, isopsuchos, ekpsuchō, apopsuchō, apsuchos, eupsucheō), and miscellaneous bleed (qum sub-glosses, me.eh, me.a, be.li, ra.phah, na.phash, no.phet, la.vav, lib.bev, le.vi.vah, bal, lev H3821, qa.ma). These are all properly extracted or analysed in their home registries. None carry primary spirit content beyond the cross-registry reference already established in the CRL entries. H5314 na.phash (be refreshed) is soul vocabulary. G4894 suneidō, G0039G hagion (Holy Place) is a place term not a spirit term.

set.exclusion_reason: Cross-registry bleed. Home registry analysis covers spirit-boundary content via CRL.

operations.description: Confirm candidate_delete for all remaining candidate_delete terms (except G4152 pneumatikos, handled in OP-004). Confirmed as bleed/non-primary. Notable: G4154 pneō (to blow) is wind vocabulary but adequately covered by H7307H ru.ach:breath analysis. G0040H hagios:saint adequately covered by G0040G hagios. G4152 pneumatikos excepted — see OP-004.

set.exclusion_reason: Confirmed candidate_delete. Bleed vocabulary. See individual notes.

active_terms_post_patch: G4151H pneuma breath/wind (341v — shared pool with G4151G)
reclassified: H7307I ru.ach:side → candidate_delete (spatial preposition, no spirit content)
G4152 pneumatikos → extracted (essential for two-modes structure, needs verse data)
notes: G4151G and G4151H share the same 341-verse pool (211 verses overlap). Unique verses ~471.
H7307J ru.ach:temper is SHARED_TERM with anger (reg 4) — confirmed cross-registry.
G4152 pneumatikos requires verse data before Session B closure — Claude Code action needed.
H5397 ne.sha.mah corrected from candidate_delete to extracted — Gen 2:7 is the creation-breath anchor.
